created_after.description: Restrict results to all records created after the given date.
created_after.name: created_after

created_before.description: Restrict results to all records created before the given date.
created_before.name: created_before

full_names.description: Comma separated list of full names of products or product-application pairs
full_names.name: full_names

fullname.description: The full name of the record
fullname.name: fullname

limit_scope.description: Restrict results to records belonging to given Applications and Services.
limit_scope.name: limit_scope

mine.description: If true, restricts output to records owned by the requesting user.
mine.name: mine

order_by.description:
Specifies the order by criteria. The syntax of this parameter is
similar to the syntax of the _order by_ clause of an SQL statement.
We support ordering by these fields:
- id
- service_id
- application_id
- created_at
- updated_at
- status
- name
- kind
- type
- service
- endpoint
- status_type
- current_status
- last_ping_at
- status_updated_at
- aged
- child_service_id
- parent_service_id

You can also specify asc/desc order

```sql
name asc
```

Or in order to retrieve all service ordered by name _and_ type:

```sql
name asc, type desc
```

If the parameter isn't provided, or if the value is empty, then
no explicit ordering will be applied.
order_by.name: order_by

page.description: Page number of record list when record list exceeds specified page size
page.name: page

product_ids.description: Comma separated list of product ids
product_ids.name: product_ids

resource_type.description: If provided, restricts output to records of this type.
resource_type.name: resource_type

size.description: Maximum number of records to return
size.name: size
